props.message:
The Library of Past Echoes.

Keep your voice low. The pages remember footfalls.

Posted, in a careful hand:

"Silence is asked of every walker in the Stacks. The deepest reading is what looks back at you. Do not surprise it."

An older notice, the ink running:

"Three Readers were lost to a long inscription in the year of the second thinning. Their names are kept in the west aisle. Speak them only when alone."

Roster of the Readers, freshly inked:

Mistress Yvenn (Head)
Apprentice Yawen
Pell (sabbatical)
Theln (Wyrdmark records)
Olm (the Drawing-In)
— and one entry, scratched out long ago.

A page-marker pinned to the shelf:

"What is forgotten was loved by something. The Library remembers on its behalf."

An inscription carved into the lintel above the north stair:

"All who walk here walk in Lirien's breath. Walk softly. The breath is thin in the next room."

A folded notice, weighted with a stone:

"Climbing of ladders by visitors is permitted. Climbing of shelves is not. The shelves were not built to be climbed and they remember."

A small wood plaque:

"In memory of the year of the long quiet, when no Reader spoke for a season, that the pages might be heard."

A reader's marginalia, framed and posted:

"The deepest reading is what looks back at you. I have read this line three times this week and it has read me twice. — Olm"

A note tied to a lamp-post with a faded cord:

"To whomever oils these lamps: the third from the south flickers when something old is being read aloud. Please do not adjust it. It is doing its work."

props.open_message: A handful of small smooth stones, kept for some reading-tally long forgotten.
A glass vessel. Inside, a small bright thing turns once and looks at you.
A pressed heart-flower from the Sourceplain, dry as paper, still warm to the touch.
A small brass key. The Library keeps a few of these for its inner aisles.

root.speaker: Mistress Yvenn
root.text: Welcome to the Library of Past Echoes, little walker. Speak softly. The old voices listen here.

choices.label: Have you read of the Sigils?

mission.speaker: Mistress Yvenn
mission.text: We keep what was, so what is can know it was real. That is the whole of our craft, said simply. Said well, it would take a longer life than mine.

choices.label: Have you read of the Sigils?

readers.speaker: Mistress Yvenn
readers.text: Readers. We do not call ourselves scholars. A scholar adds. A Reader only listens. Apprentice Yawen is somewhere up a ladder. Pell is back from the field, though he will not say from where.

others.speaker: Mistress Yvenn
others.text: Theln tends the Wyrdmark records. Olm has bent his life around the Drawing-In, the poor soul. And there is one more, who comes and goes. We do not name her aloud.

cat_unspoken.speaker: Mistress Yvenn
cat_unspoken.text: She is a Reader who left, and came back. That is all I will say of her, and all you should ask.

sigils.speaker: Mistress Yvenn
sigils.text: Three marks of the Source. Lirien's Sight. Khorgaul's Shaping. And the third — Sharing — has not walked the realm in a long age. We keep the records, and we wait.

drawing.speaker: Mistress Yvenn
drawing.text: Khorgaul pulls. The Source thins. We catalogue the thinning here, page by page. By the Source, I had hoped to be retired before the shelves grew this heavy.

slip.speaker: Mistress Yvenn
slip.text: Faring? Oh — better, I think, than when the breath nearly stilled last week. We had three Readers in the south aisle holding hands when it happened. A bad hour. But the breath came back.

deeper.speaker: Mistress Yvenn
deeper.text: Down: the Cache, where memories fade. The Stacks east, where they are kept. The Ledger west, where each day is written. And the Backwater, beyond — the stream that overflows toward Mirelake.

bye.speaker: Mistress Yvenn
bye.text: Walk well, little walker. Tread softly. The pages remember footfalls.

root.speaker: Apprentice Yawen
root.text: Oh — hello! Do you need a book? I can find a book. Probably. I'm still learning the aisles.

mistress.speaker: Apprentice Yawen
mistress.text: Mistress Yvenn? Oh, she knows everything. I mean — everything that's been written. She remembers shelves the way I remember songs. I want to be like her one day. If I last that long.

duties.speaker: Apprentice Yawen
duties.text: I dust. I copy out faded lines. I climb a lot of ladders. The Mistress says reading is mostly climbing, in the end.

joke.speaker: Apprentice Yawen
joke.text: Lirien's breath, no! I'd be three hundred. Pell laughs at me when I try.

bye.speaker: Apprentice Yawen
bye.text: Walk well! Mind the third ladder, it wobbles.

root.speaker: Reader-on-Sabbatical Pell
root.text: Hm. A walker. Don't mind me. I'm not on duty. I'm — between things.

field.speaker: Reader-on-Sabbatical Pell
field.text: Out. In the realm. Reading the kind of pages that aren't bound. I'd rather not say where, not yet. Some things you say aloud and they stop being true.

book.speaker: Reader-on-Sabbatical Pell
book.text: (He keeps one hand flat on the cover, as if it might open if he stopped.) It's not for reading. Not by me. Not yet.

bye.speaker: Reader-on-Sabbatical Pell
bye.text: Walk well. And don't read in straight lines. Nothing useful is ever in a straight line.

root.speaker: Reader Theln
root.text: You smell of the outside. Good. Come, come — I work the Wyrdmark records, the small ones in the alcove down at the Cache. Have you been to the Hollow yet?

hollow.speaker: Reader Theln
hollow.text: The Hollow of the Last Wyrd. Old. Old past my reading. Down through the Cache and into the small alcove there — Recorder Thav can tell you more than I can. He keeps the closer pages.

worry.speaker: Reader Theln
worry.text: The Hollow has been louder, of late. Pages move on the desk between morning and morning. Khorgaul's grip, I would rather it stayed quiet, the way old things should.

wyrdking.speaker: Reader Theln
wyrdking.text: The Wyrdking Bonelord. He sits at the bottom of the Hollow and does not want to. Khorgaul shaped him from the bones of the Last Wyrd, and the bones do not forget being a creature.

bye.speaker: Reader Theln
bye.text: Walk well. And if you go to the Hollow — go with a Sigil. Not without.

root.speaker: Reader Olm
root.text: Sit, walk on, do as you like. I am reading. Always reading. The Drawing-In is my whole shelf and I have not finished a page in three years.

what.speaker: Reader Olm
what.text: The slow loss. The Source pulled out of glades, out of doors, out of the names of streams. A thinning. You will not see it. You will only feel something missing, where something used to lean toward you.

khorgaul.speaker: Reader Olm
khorgaul.text: The Hoarder. Bearer of the Sigil of Shaping. He gathers. He has been gathering for a long age, and a long age is a long time to gather.

why.speaker: Reader Olm
why.text: Because the Sigil of Shaping wants substance, and the substance is nearby, and he is hungry. He does not hate the Wyrdmark. That is the saddest part of any reading I have done.

done.speaker: Reader Olm
done.text: The Sigil of Sharing. Lirien's third mark. It returns what is held. It has not walked the realm in a hundred years. We wait. We have always waited.

bye.speaker: Reader Olm
bye.text: Walk well. Read what you can. Forget what you must.

root.speaker: Cat
root.text: (She glances at you sideways. She touches the wall beside a sign. A single line of an old text comes out of her, without her wanting it to.) ...the lamp does not know it is a lamp.

who.speaker: Cat
who.text: A Reader who left, and came back. That is all anyone says of me. They are right not to say more.

said.speaker: Cat
said.text: (She looks at her hand, surprised.) The wall said it. I only carried it the rest of the way out.

bye.speaker: Cat
bye.text: Walk well. The pages already know you walked.

props.warp_message: The dust stirs. The Echoes have catalogued your passage.

root.speaker: Apprentice Ted
root.text: Oh — oh, hello. Hello! I'm — I'm cataloguing. I'm meant to be cataloguing. Mistress Yvenn said one shelf a day and I've done — *(he glances at the shelf)* — about half a shelf. In two days. Please don't tell her.

trade.speaker: Apprentice Ted
trade.text:
*he reaches for the scroll, then stops himself, then reaches again.*

May I — may I see — oh. OH. This is — the hand is — this is from the Reader-on-Sabbatical's circuit, I'd swear it. Look, look, the way the loops carry through. This is a FIND, walker, this is a — a real one.

*he scrambles in a drawer, pulls out a length of pale-dyed ribbon, presses it into your hand with both of his.*

Take this. Please. It's not — it's only a ribbon, but it's a good one, the dye's true, my sister made it before — before. Take it. Walk it somewhere. The scroll stays here where it belongs and I'll — I'll catalogue it FIRST, I promise, before the shelf, even.

thanks.speaker: Apprentice Ted
thanks.text:
*he is already unrolling the scroll on the desk, very carefully, with both hands.*

Walk well, walker. Walk well. Oh, this is a GOOD day.

already.speaker: Apprentice Ted
already.text: Still carrying the ribbon? Good. Good! It wants — it wants to be given properly. You'll know who. I think you'll know.

slow.speaker: Apprentice Ted
slow.text: Every book makes me read three more. I open a thing meant for the catalogue and the catalogue waits and the THING — the thing pulls me in. The Mistress says that's the trade. The Mistress says nobody good at this is fast.

bye.speaker: Apprentice Ted
bye.text: Walk well! Mind the — the third ladder, Yawen says it wobbles. I haven't tried it yet.
